User Story: As authenticated user, I want to generate design image.
Expected Behaviour: Generate an AI design image using Gemini 3 Pro Image Preview. **Requires authentication** (Supabase Bearer token). **Modes:** - `text-to-image`: Generate from a text prompt - `image-to-image`: Transform an uploaded image with a prompt **Request body:** - `mode`: Generation mode - `prompt`: Text description (3-4000 chars) - `image`: Base64-encoded source image (required for image-to-image) - `mimeType`: MIME type of source image (e.g., image/jpeg) **Response:** - `success`: Whether generation succeeded - `image`: Base64 data URL of generated image (on success) - `error`: Error message (on failure) - `code`: Error code (on failure)

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to send agent chat message.
Expected Behaviour: Stream an AI agent response via Server-Sent Events. The main-pool DB session is released after the initial conversation setup. The streaming phase uses a background-pool session so the main pool is not held for the entire AI response duration.

User Story: As public, I want to send public agent chat message.
Expected Behaviour: Stream an AI agent response for unauthenticated guests via SSE. Only property search tools are available. No conversation persistence. The main-pool DB session is released before streaming begins and a background-pool session is used for tool calls, matching the pattern in the authenticated ``/chat`` endpoint.

User Story: As public, I want to public auth configuration (Google client IDs).
Expected Behaviour: PUBLIC. Return Google OAuth client IDs (any may be null).

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to permanently delete the current user's account.
Expected Behaviour: AUTH required. Permanently delete the caller's own account. Hard-deletes the Supabase Auth user (revoking all sessions) and anonymizes + soft-deletes the local record. App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) compliance: the account becomes permanently unusable. Returns 204 No Content (alternate mobile-friendly route; the canonical ``DELETE /users/me`` returns 200 + MessageResponse). [HTTP 204 on success]

User Story: As public, I want to probe the auth status of an email/phone (drives client login flow).
Expected Behaviour: PUBLIC. Return a NEUTRAL status for the given identifier. Rate-limited per-IP. Detects channel (``'@'`` → email, else phone), looks the identifier up directly in Supabase ``auth.users``, and computes ``next_step``: ``"password"`` iff the identifier exists, is verified, and has a password credential (``encrypted_password`` present); otherwise ``"otp"``.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to record the last authentication method used by the current user.
Expected Behaviour: AUTH required. Persist ``method`` on the current user. Returns 204 No Content. [HTTP 204 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to link an OAuth identity to the current Supabase user.
Expected Behaviour: AUTH required. Wrap the GoTrue Admin identity-linking call.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get current user auth state.
Expected Behaviour: Compute and return the current auth gate stage for the user. Returns the centralized gate state: ``stage``: one of identifier_verification, password_setup, profile_completion, app_onboarding, active ``next_action``: what the client should route to ``missing_fields``: profile fields still required (if applicable) The ``app`` query param selects which app's onboarding check to run (defaults to ``flatmates``). New apps register their check via :func:`register_app_onboarding_check` during startup.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to upload user avatar.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to complete user onboarding.
Expected Behaviour: Mark the given app's onboarding as complete. Sets ``<app>_onboarding_completed = True`` on the current user so the gate advances past ``app_onboarding`` on subsequent ``GET /me/auth-state`` calls.

Expected Behaviour: Upload a single file with MediaFile tracking. Files are uploaded to user-scoped paths: users/{user_id}/...

Expected Behaviour: Upload multiple files in a single request. Files are uploaded to user-scoped paths: users/{user_id}/...

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to confirm upload.
Expected Behaviour: Confirm a client-side upload completed successfully. Call this endpoint after uploading a file directly to storage using the signed URL from /presigned. This verifies the file exists and updates the MediaFile record status from 'pending' to 'complete'.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to list media files.
Expected Behaviour: List uploaded media files for the current user.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to bulk delete media files.
Expected Behaviour: Delete up to 50 media files in a single request. Only media owned by the current user are deleted. IDs that are not found or are owned by another user are returned in the ``failed`` list.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get media file.
Expected Behaviour: Get a single media file for the current user.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to delete media file.
Expected Behaviour: Delete a media file and attempt to remove the underlying object from storage. [HTTP 204 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update media file.
Expected Behaviour: Update media processing status or URLs.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to create presigned uploads.
Expected Behaviour: Create presigned upload URLs for direct client-side uploads. Returns signed URLs that clients can use to upload files directly to storage. After uploading, clients should call POST /upload/confirm/{upload_id} to confirm the upload completed successfully. Files are stored in user-scoped paths based on folder_type: - AVATAR: users/{user_id}/avatars/ - PROPERTY_IMAGE: users/{user_id}/properties/{property_id}/images/ - PROPERTY_VIDEO: users/{user_id}/properties/{property_id}/videos/ - TOUR/SCENE: users/{user_id}/tours/{tour_id}/scenes/{scene_id}/ - DOCUMENT_*: users/{user_id}/documents/{type}/ - GENERIC: users/{user_id}/uploads/

User Story: As public, I want to analyze floor plan vastu.
Expected Behaviour: Analyze a floor plan image for Vastu Shastra compliance. This is a **public endpoint** - no authentication required. Upload a floor plan image and receive a comprehensive Vastu analysis including: - Overall Vastu score (1-10) - Room-by-room analysis with status ratings - Major defects identified with severity levels - Practical remedies and improvement suggestions - Full markdown report for display/download **Supported image formats:** JPEG, PNG, WebP (max 5MB) **North Direction Options:** - `up`: North is at the top of the image - `down`: North is at the bottom - `left`: North is to the left - `right`: North is to the right - `unknown`: Not sure (AI will attempt to detect) **AI Provider Options:** - `glm`: ZhipuAI GLM-5V-Turbo (default) - `gemini`: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite

User Story: As public, I want to vastu health check.
Expected Behaviour: Health check endpoint for the Vastu analyzer service.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to list AI jobs.
Expected Behaviour: List AI processing jobs for the current user. Returns jobs with optional status filtering and cursor pagination.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get AI job.
Expected Behaviour: Get the status and result of an AI processing job. Returns the job details including progress and results if completed.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to cancel AI job.
Expected Behaviour: Cancel a running AI processing job. Only pending or processing jobs can be cancelled.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to analyze scene.
Expected Behaviour: Analyze a single scene using AI. Returns room type, suggested title/description, and quality assessment.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to apply hotspot suggestions.
Expected Behaviour: Apply AI hotspot suggestions to a scene. Creates hotspots based on the selected suggestion IDs.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to generate scene description.
Expected Behaviour: Generate AI description for a scene. Creates a compelling description based on the panorama content.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to suggest scene hotspots.
Expected Behaviour: Get AI-suggested hotspots for a scene. Analyzes the panorama to suggest optimal navigation and info hotspot placements.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to generate tour.
Expected Behaviour: Generate a new tour from uploaded 360 images using AI. Accepts multipart/form-data with image files and tour options. Images are uploaded to storage and then processed by AI to: - Detect room types - Generate scene titles and descriptions - Optionally suggest hotspot placements

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to analyze tour scenes.
Expected Behaviour: Analyze all scenes in a tour using AI. Detects room types, suggests titles/descriptions, and evaluates image quality. Returns a job ID for tracking progress.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to apply scene analysis.
Expected Behaviour: Apply AI scene analysis suggestions to scenes. Updates scene titles and descriptions based on selected suggestions.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to generate tour descriptions.
Expected Behaviour: Generate AI descriptions for all scenes in a tour. Creates descriptions for each scene based on panorama content.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to suggest tour hotspots.
Expected Behaviour: Get AI-suggested hotspots for all scenes in a tour. Analyzes all panoramas and suggests navigation hotspots to connect scenes.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to optimize tour.
Expected Behaviour: Optimize an existing tour using AI suggestions.

Expected Behaviour: List all custom domains for the current user.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get custom domain.
Expected Behaviour: Get a specific custom domain by ID.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to delete custom domain.
Expected Behaviour: Delete a custom domain. [HTTP 204 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to verify custom domain.
Expected Behaviour: Trigger verification for a custom domain. Checks if the DNS TXT record has been added correctly. Returns verification status and instructions if not yet verified.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get hotspot.
Expected Behaviour: Get a hotspot by ID.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update hotspot.
Expected Behaviour: Update a hotspot's properties (partial update). Can update type, position, target scene, title, description, icon, and custom data. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated. Note: Both PUT and PATCH are supported for backward compatibility. PATCH is the recommended method for partial updates.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update hotspot.
Expected Behaviour: Update a hotspot's properties (partial update). Can update type, position, target scene, title, description, icon, and custom data. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated. Note: Both PUT and PATCH are supported for backward compatibility. PATCH is the recommended method for partial updates.

Expected Behaviour: Delete a hotspot from a scene. [HTTP 204 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update hotspot position.
Expected Behaviour: Update only the position of a hotspot. This is a convenience endpoint for drag-and-drop positioning in the editor.

User Story: As public, I want to get public tour.
Expected Behaviour: Get a publicly accessible tour by ID. This endpoint is used by the public viewer and embed page. It does not require authentication but the tour must be: - Published (status = 'published') - Public (is_public = True) - Not deleted Optionally tracks view analytics (disable with track=false query param). Returns the complete tour structure including scenes and hotspots, ordered by scene order_index.

User Story: As public, I want to track tour event.
Expected Behaviour: Track an analytics event for a public tour. Event types: - view: Tour was loaded - scene_view: A specific scene was viewed - hotspot_click: A hotspot was clicked - share: Tour was shared - fullscreen: Fullscreen mode was toggled - vr_enter: VR mode was entered This endpoint does not require authentication.

User Story: As public, I want to like tour.
Expected Behaviour: Increment the like count for a public tour. Rate-limited per IP to prevent count manipulation.

User Story: As public, I want to unlike tour.
Expected Behaviour: Decrement the like count for a public tour. Rate-limited per IP to prevent count manipulation.

User Story: As public, I want to get public tour scenes.
Expected Behaviour: Get all scenes for a publicly accessible tour. Returns scenes ordered by order_index with their hotspots.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get scene.
Expected Behaviour: Get a scene by ID with all its hotspots.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update scene.
Expected Behaviour: Update a scene's details. Can update title, description, image URLs, and metadata.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update scene.
Expected Behaviour: Update a scene's details. Can update title, description, image URLs, and metadata.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to delete scene.
Expected Behaviour: Delete a scene from a tour. This will also delete all hotspots associated with the scene. [HTTP 204 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to list scene hotspots.
Expected Behaviour: List all hotspots for a scene. Returns hotspots ordered by their order_index.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to create scene hotspot.
Expected Behaviour: Add a new hotspot to a scene. Hotspots can be navigation links, info popups, or media elements. [HTTP 201 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to list tours.
Expected Behaviour: List all tours for the current user. Returns paginated list of tours with optional filtering by status and search.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to create tour.
Expected Behaviour: Create a new virtual tour. Creates a tour in draft status. Add scenes and hotspots before publishing. [HTTP 201 on success]

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to get tour.
Expected Behaviour: Get a tour by ID with all scenes and hotspots. Returns the complete tour structure including nested scenes and their hotspots.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update tour.
Expected Behaviour: Update a tour's details. Can update title, description, settings, and other tour properties.

User Story: As authenticated user, I want to update tour.
Expected Behaviour: Update a tour's details. Can update title, description, settings, and other tour properties.

Expected Behaviour: Delete a tour (soft delete). The tour is marked as deleted but not permanently removed from the database. [HTTP 204 on success]

Expected Behaviour: Get analytics data for a tour. Returns view counts, engagement metrics, device breakdown, and daily views.

Expected Behaviour: Publish a tour to make it publicly accessible. Sets the tour status to 'published' and records the publish timestamp.

Expected Behaviour: Reorder scenes within a tour. Provide the scene IDs in the desired order.

Expected Behaviour: Unpublish a tour to make it private again. Sets the tour status back to 'draft'.
